# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

class ApplicationMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com")
  layout "mailer"
end

class SendgridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  SENDGRID_API_KEY = ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"]
  SENDGRID_USERNAME = "apikey".freeze
  SENDGRID_PASSWORD = SENDGRID_API_KEY

  default delivery_method_options: {
    address: "smtp.sendgrid.net",
    port: 587,
    domain: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_DOMAIN", "localhost"),
    user_name: SENDGRID_USERNAME,
    password: SENDGRID_PASSWORD,
    authentication: :plain,
    enable_starttls_auto: true
  }

  before_action :configure_delivery_method

  def send_email(to:, subject:, body:, from: nil, cc: nil, bcc: nil, reply_to: nil, content_type: "text/plain")
    mail(
      to: to,
      from: from || default_params[:from],
      cc: cc,
      bcc: bcc,
      reply_to: reply_to,
      subject: subject,
      content_type: content_type
    ) do |format|
      if content_type.to_s.include?("html")
        format.html { render html: body.html_safe }
      else
        format.text { render plain: body }
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def configure_delivery_method
    raise "SENDGRID_API_KEY is not set" if SENDGRID_PASSWORD.to_s.strip.empty?

    mail.delivery_method.settings.merge!(
      address: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_ADDRESS", "smtp.sendgrid.net"),
      port: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_PORT", 587).to_i,
      domain: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_DOMAIN", "localhost"),
      user_name: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_USERNAME", SENDGRID_USERNAME),
      password: SENDGRID_PASSWORD,
      authentication: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_AUTHENTICATION", "plain").to_sym,
      enable_starttls_auto: ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_ENABLE_STARTTLS_AUTO", true)),
      openssl_verify_mode: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_OPENSSL_VERIFY_MODE", "peer")
    )
  end
end

Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
  config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
    address: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_ADDRESS", "smtp.sendgrid.net"),
    port: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_PORT", 587).to_i,
    domain: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_DOMAIN", "localhost"),
    user_name: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_USERNAME", "apikey"),
    password: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_API_KEY", ""),
    authentication: :plain,
    enable_starttls_auto: ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_ENABLE_STARTTLS_AUTO", true)),
    openssl_verify_mode: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_OPENSSL_VERIFY_MODE", "peer")
  }
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = {
    host: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_HOST", "localhost"),
    protocol: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_PROTOCOL", "http")
  }
end
